original_abstract: Hydrogen peroxide was produced as a solar fuel from water and dioxygen using solar energy by combination of a water oxidation catalyst and a photocatalyst for two-electron reduction of O2 in acidic aqueous solutions. Photocatalytic production of H2O2 occurred under photoirradiation of [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ (Me2phen = 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline) used as a photocatalyst with visible light in the presence of Ir(OH)3 acting as a water oxidation catalyst in an O2-saturated H2SO4 aqueous solution. Photoinduced electron transfer from the excited state of [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ to O2 results in the formation of [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ and a superoxide radical anion (O2˙−) which is protonated to produce H2O2via disproportionation of HO2˙ in competition with back electron transfer (BET) from O2˙− to [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+. [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ oxidises water with the aid of catalysis of Ir(OH)3 to produce O2. The photocatalytic reactivity of H2O2 production was improved by replacing Ir(OH)3 nanoparticles by [CoIII(Cp*)(bpy)(H2O)]2+ in the presence of Sc(NO3)3 in water. The optimised quantum yield of the photocatalytic H2O2 production at λ = 450 nm was determined using a ferrioxalate actinometer to be 37%. The value of conversion efficiency from solar energy to chemical energy was also determined to be 0.25%.
humanized_abstract: Solar production of hydrogen peroxide as a solar fuel from water and dioxygen was achieved using a water oxidation catalyst and a photocatalyst for the two-electron reduction of dioxygen to produce H2O2 in acidic aqueous solutions. Here we report that photocatalytic production of H2O2 was achieved under photoirradiation with the visible light of a Ru(II) complex, [RuII(Me2phen)3)]2+ (Me2phen = 4,7-dimethyl-1,10-phenanthroline) using the solar energy while an Ir(OH)3 was used as a water oxidation catalyst in an O2-saturated aqueous solution of H2SO4. Photoinduced reduction of dioxygen proceeded via electron transfer to the excited state of the [RuII(Me2phen)3]2+ to produce the Ru(III) species [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ along with the superoxide radical anion (O2˙−) of which the protonation and subsequent disproportionation of HO2˙ leads to H2O2. Formation of O2 took place through oxidation of water by [RuIII(Me2phen)3]3+ with the aid of Ir(OH)3 catalyst. The photocatalytic reactivity of production of H2O2 was enhanced in the presence of [CoIII(Cp*)(bpy)(H2O)]2+ in place of Ir(OH)3 catalyst nanoparticles in the presence of Sc(NO3)3 in water to produce 62 mm H2O2 under illumination at room temperature. The optimized quantum yield of production of H2O2 at λ = 450 nm in aqueous solution of H2SO4 was determined using a ferrioxalate actinometer to be 37%. The conversion efficiency of solar energy into chemical energy is calculated to be 0.25%.
